verses.commentary: Separation for communion with the Father. Pattern of retreat after conflict.
Official teaching role. Public ministry in sacred space.
Public humiliation. Setup for a trap. Woman is pawn, not person.
Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery. Precise accusation. Evidence certain. Where is the man? Law requires both parties (Leviticus 20:10).
In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do You say? Deuteronomy 22:22-24. Trap set. If Jesus shows mercy, He opposes Moses. If He condemns, He contradicts His grace.
Silent response. Writing recalls God's finger on stone tablets (Exodus 31:18). What did He write? Speculation endless. Perhaps sins of accusers.

When they kept on questioning Him, He straightened up and said to them, Let any one of you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her. Brilliant reversal. Not mercy versus law, but qualified executioners. Sinlessness required. Self-examination demanded.
Silence creates space for conscience.
Age brings awareness of accumulated sin. Gradual exit. Trap collapses. Accusers become the accused.
Jesus straightened up and asked her, Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you? Gentle inquiry. He knows the answer. Draws her into conversation.

No one, sir. Then neither do I condemn you. Go now and leave your life of sin. Grace and truth. No condemnation, but no license. Forgiveness includes call to transformation. Sin acknowledged but future changed.

When Jesus spoke again to the people, He said, I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life. Second 'I AM' statement. Light imagery from Tabernacles' candles. Jesus is revelation, guidance, truth. Following brings illumination.
The Pharisees challenged Him, Here You are, appearing as Your own witness; Your testimony is not valid. Legal objection. Self-testimony inadmissible. Deuteronomy 19:15 requires two witnesses.

Even if I testify on My own behalf, My testimony is valid, for I know where I came from and where I am going. But you have no idea where I come from or where I am going. Self-knowledge validates. Origin and destiny known. They judge without understanding.
Jesus does not judge now; He came to save (John 3:17). Judgment awaits appointed time.
United verdict. Divine authority.
Meets the requirement.
I am one who testifies for Myself; My other witness is the Father, who sent Me. Two witnesses: Jesus and the Father. Sufficient legal validation. Divine dual testimony.

Then they asked Him, Where is Your father? You do not know Me or My Father. If you knew Me, you would know My Father also. Misunderstanding. They think earthly father. Jesus speaks of God. Knowing Jesus means knowing the Father. They know neither.
Treasury, public space. Divine timing protects. They cannot act prematurely.

Once more Jesus said to them, I am going away, and you will look for Me, and you will die in your sin. Where I go, you cannot come. Departure announced. Future regret. Death in sin means eternal separation. Heaven inaccessible to unbelievers.
This made the Jews ask, Will He kill Himself? Is that why He says, Where I go, you cannot come? Grotesque misunderstanding. Suicide was considered shameful. They think the worst.
But He continued, You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world. Fundamental difference. Origin determines nature. Earthly versus heavenly. Worldly versus divine.

I told you that you would die in your sins; if you do not believe that I am He, you will indeed die in your sins. Egō eimi (ἐγώ εἰμι). Divine name invoked. Belief in His deity essential. Unbelief brings death. Identity is salvific issue.
Who are You? Just what I have been telling you from the beginning. Identity question persists. Jesus points to consistency. His teaching has been clear. They choose not to hear.

I have much to say in judgment of you. But He who sent Me is trustworthy, and what I have heard from Him I tell the world. Restraint in judgment. Could say more. Father's reliability grounds Jesus' message. Faithful transmission.
Father language missed. Spiritual deafness.

So Jesus said, When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am He and that I do nothing on My own but speak just what the Father has taught Me. Lifted up: crucifixion and exaltation. The cross will reveal. Divine identity proven through death. Submission to the Father demonstrated.
The One who sent Me is with Me; He has not left Me alone, for I always do what pleases Him. Constant presence. Perfect obedience. Unbroken fellowship with the Father. Approval continuous.
Faith emerges. Truth penetrates. Yet belief will be tested (following verses).

To the Jews who had believed Him, Jesus said, If you hold to My teaching, you are really My disciples. Abiding in the word distinguishes true disciples. Continuance proves genuineness. Faith that endures is real.
Then you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free. Knowledge through obedience. Truth liberates. Freedom from sin, error, bondage. Progressive revelation to faithful disciples.

They answered Him, We are Abraham's descendants and have never been slaves of anyone. How can You say that we shall be set free? Ethnic pride. Delusional claim. Egypt, Assyria, Babylon, Persia, Greece, Rome all enslaved Israel. Present Roman occupation ignored.
Very truly I tell you, everyone who sins is a slave to sin. Slavery is moral, not political. Sin enslaves internally. Habitual sin creates bondage. Spiritual captivity more serious than physical.
Hagar's son expelled. Sonship brings inheritance. Slavery is temporary status; sonship is permanent.
Jesus' power to emancipate. Complete freedom, not partial. Real versus nominal.
But spiritual kinship absent. Murder proves alien hearts. His word finds no welcome.

I am telling you what I have seen in the Father's presence, and you are doing what you have heard from your father. Contrasting fathers. Jesus speaks from divine vision. They act from their father's character. Like father, like children.

Abraham is our father. If you were Abraham's children, then you would do what Abraham did. They claim the patriarch. Jesus denies spiritual lineage. True children imitate forefather. Abraham believed God; they reject God's Son.
Abraham welcomed divine messengers (Genesis 18). They plot to kill God's Messenger.

You are doing the works of your own father. We are not illegitimate children. The only Father we have is God Himself. Implicit slur against Jesus' virgin birth. They claim divine paternity. Jesus will expose the lie.

If God were your Father, you would love Me, for I have come here from God. I have not come on My own; God sent Me. Love for Jesus proves love for God. Rejection of Son proves rejection of Father. Divine mission authenticated.
Inability, not just unwillingness. Moral incapacity to comprehend.
Satan is their true father. Murder and lies characterize both. Cain killed Abel (1 John 3:12). Devil's nature: deception and death. Native language: falsehood.
Truth should produce belief. Instead, it produces rejection. Preference for lies over truth.
No one can accuse Him. Perfect life validates His claims. Unbelief is irrational given His character.

Whoever belongs to God hears what God says. The reason you do not hear is that you do not belong to God. Hearing proves paternity. God's children recognize His voice. Their deafness exposes alien origin.

The Jews answered Him, Aren't we right in saying that You are a Samaritan and demon-possessed? Ethnic slur and spiritual accusation. Samaritan: half-breed, heretic. Demon-possessed: controlled by evil. Desperate slander.
I am not possessed by a demon, but I honor My Father and you dishonor Me. Calm denial. Redirects to honor. He exalts the Father; they dishonor the Son. Contrasting responses to deity.
The Father vindicates. God judges those who reject His Son.
Very truly I tell you, whoever obeys My word will never see death. Eternal life through obedience. Keeping His word prevents spiritual death. Physical death transcended.

At this they exclaimed, Now we know that You are demon-possessed! Abraham died and so did the prophets, yet You say that whoever obeys Your word will never taste death. Outrage at the claim. They cite universal mortality. Abraham and prophets died. How can Jesus' followers avoid death?

Are You greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do You think You are? The question is correct. Yes, He is greater. Who is He? God incarnate. They ask the right question with the wrong heart.

If I glorify Myself, My glory means nothing. My Father, whom you claim as your God, is the One who glorifies Me. Self-glorification is empty. The Father glorifies the Son. Their God is His Father. Ironic disconnect.
They claim but do not know God. Jesus knows intimately. To deny would make Him a liar like them. Knowledge proven by obedience.

Your father Abraham rejoiced at the thought of seeing My day; he saw it and was glad. Abraham foresaw Christ. Genesis 22: Isaac's deliverance foreshadowed. Melchizedek encounter. Divine promises pointed to Messiah. Abraham's faith looked forward.
You are not yet fifty years old, and You have seen Abraham! Age objection. Jesus appears mid-thirties. Chronological impossibility cited. They think He claims physical encounter.
Very truly I tell you, before Abraham was born, I am! Egō eimi (ἐγώ εἰμι). Exodus 3:14. Divine name claimed. Eternal preexistence. Not 'I was' but 'I AM.' Timeless existence. Supreme claim to deity.
Leviticus 24:16. They understood His claim. Attempted execution. Divine protection. He vanishes. The hour not yet come.
